24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:ab_setup}
26	
27	A regular projective surface \(S\) over a field --- such as \(\mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1\)
28	or the product \(C \times C\) of a smooth projective curve \(C\) with itself --- has a regular
29	local ring at every (closed) point, of Krull dimension \(2\). To extend a rational map
30	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from \(S\) to an abelian variety across a codimension-\(2\) closed
31	point, one argues that the local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) at such a point has \emph{depth at
32	least} \(2\), hence a rational function with no codimension-\(1\) poles on a neighbourhood of \(x\)
33	extends across \(x\). The depth \(\geq 2\) inequality is exactly what the
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum formula gives: for a regular local ring \(R\) one has \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\),
35	so
36	\[
37	  \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{pd}_R(R) + \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{depth}(R)
38	\]
39	trivially, but combined with the regular-sequence definition of depth and the fact that a
40	regular local ring of dimension \(d\) admits an \(R\)-regular sequence of length \(d\) generating
41	the maximal ideal, one obtains \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\), i.e.\ \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay
42	(Corollary~REF below). This is the input A.4.a needs.
43	
44	This chapter packages:
45	\begin{enumerate}
46	  \item the regular-sequence definition of depth and its \(\text{Ext}\) reformulation
47	    (Definition~REF and Lemma~REF),
48	  \item the projective-dimension invariant of a finitely-generated module
49	    (Definition~REF),
50	  \item the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma
51	    (Lemma~REF),
52	  \item the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula proper
53	    (Theorem~REF), and
54	  \item the corollary regular \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay
55	    (Corollary~REF).
56	\end{enumerate}
57	A short closing paragraph (Section~REF) explains how A.4.a
58	consumes Corollary~REF to push a codim-1 extension across a
59	codim-2 point.
60	
61	\section{Depth of a module over a local ring}
62	\label{sec:ab_depth}
63	
64	\begin{definition}
65	
66	\leanok
67	  [Depth of a module]
68	  \label{def:depth}
69	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth}
70	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth)
71	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17748-L17759).
72	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
73	  % "Let \(R\) be a ring, and \(I \subset R\) an ideal. Let \(M\) be a finite \(R\)-module.
74	  %  The {\it \(I\)-depth} of \(M\), denoted \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is defined as follows:
75	  %  \begin{enumerate}
76	  %  \item if \(IM \not = M\), then \(\text{depth}_I(M)\) is the supremum in
77	  %  \(\{0, 1, 2, \ldots, \infty\}\) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences in \(I\),
78	  %  \item if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty\).
79	  %  \end{enumerate}
80	  %  If \((R, \mathfrak m)\) is local we call \(\text{depth}_{\mathfrak m}(M)\) simply
81	  %  the {\it depth} of \(M\)."
82	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth).}
83	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(I \subseteq R\) be an ideal, and let \(M\) be a finitely
84	  generated \(R\)-module. The \emph{\(I\)-depth} of \(M\), written \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is the
85	  supremum in \(\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}\) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences contained
86	  in \(I\), provided \(IM \neq M\); if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty\). When
87	  \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is a Noetherian local ring we abbreviate
88	  \(\text{depth}(M) := \text{depth}_{\mathfrak{m}}(M)\) and call this simply the
89	  \emph{depth} of \(M\). The depth of the ring \(R\) itself is \(\text{depth}(R)\), the depth
90	  of \(R\) as a module over itself.
91	\end{definition}
92	
93	Recall that a sequence \(f_1, \dots, f_r \in R\) is called \emph{\(M\)-regular} if
94	\(M/(f_1, \dots, f_r)M \neq 0\) and for each \(i\), the element \(f_i\) is a non-zero-divisor on
95	\(M/(f_1, \dots, f_{i-1})M\). The empty sequence is regular on \(M\) iff \(M \neq 0\) (it is
96	\emph{not} regular on the zero module, but the convention \(\text{depth}(0) = \infty\)
97	above is the usual one).
98	
99	The two practical computations of depth are the regular-sequence definition (used to
100	establish a \emph{lower} bound) and the \(\text{Ext}\) characterisation (used to establish an
101	\emph{upper} bound and to prove the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequality).
102	
103	\begin{lemma}
104	
105	\leanok
106	  [Depth via \(\text{Ext}\)]
107	  \label{lem:depth_via_ext}
108	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index}
109	  
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext)
111	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17845-L17885).
112	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
113	  % "Let \(R\) be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\).
114	  %  Let \(M\) be a nonzero finite \(R\)-module. Then \(\text{depth}(M)\)
115	  %  is equal to the smallest integer \(i\) such that
116	  %  \(\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)\) is nonzero."
117	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext).}
118	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring with residue field
119	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak{m}\) and let \(M\) be a nonzero finitely generated \(R\)-module. Then
120	  \[
121	    \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \inf\bigl\{\, i \in \mathbb{N} \;\bigm|\; \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \,\bigr\}.
122	  \]
123	\end{lemma}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
126	% "Let \(\delta(M)\) denote the depth of \(M\) and let \(i(M)\) denote
127	%  the smallest integer \(i\) such that \(\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)\)
128	%  is nonzero. We will see in a moment that \(i(M) < \infty\).
129	%  By Lemma REF we have
130	%  \(\delta(M) = 0\) if and only if \(i(M) = 0\), because
131	%  \(\mathfrak m \in \text{Ass}(M)\) exactly means
132	%  that \(i(M) = 0\). Hence if \(\delta(M)\) or \(i(M)\) is \(> 0\), then we may
133	%  choose \(x \in \mathfrak m\) such that (a) \(x\) is a nonzerodivisor
134	%  on \(M\), and (b) \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta(M) - 1\).
135	%  Consider the long exact sequence
136	%  of Ext-groups associated to the short exact sequence
137	%  \(0 \to M \to M \to M/xM \to 0\) by Lemma REF:
138	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
139	%  Since \(x \in \mathfrak m\) all the maps $\Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)
140	%  \to \Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)$ are zero, see
141	%  Lemma REF.
142	%  Thus it is clear that \(i(M/xM) = i(M) - 1\). Induction on
143	%  \(\delta(M)\) finishes the proof."
144	\begin{proof}
145	  
146	  
147	  
148	  
149	\leanok
150	  Write \(\delta := \text{depth}(M)\) and \(i := \inf\{ j : \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \}\).
151	  The base case \(\delta = 0\) is exactly \(\text{Hom}_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0\) (some element of
152	  \(M\) is annihilated by \(\mathfrak{m}\), i.e.\ \(\mathfrak{m} \in \text{Ass}(M)\), i.e.\
153	  \(\mathfrak{m}\) contains a zero-divisor on \(M\)), which is \(i = 0\). For the inductive step
154	  pick \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\) a non-zero-divisor on \(M\) with
155	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta - 1\). The short exact sequence
156	  \(0 \to M \xrightarrow{x} M \to M/xM \to 0\) induces a long exact sequence of $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa,
157	  -)\(. Multiplication by \)x\( annihilates each \)\text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ (because
158	  \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\) and \(\kappa\) is annihilated by \(\mathfrak{m}\)), so the long exact
159	  sequence breaks into short pieces giving
160	  \(\text{Ext}^{j-1}_R(\kappa, M/xM) \cong \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)\) at the relevant
161	  indices, and the smallest non-vanishing index drops by \(1\). By induction
162	  \(i(M/xM) = i - 1 = \delta - 1\), so \(i = \delta\).
163	\end{proof}
164	
165	\section{Projective dimension}
166	\label{sec:ab_pd}
167	
168	\begin{definition}
169	
170	\leanok
171	  [Projective dimension]
172	  \label{def:projective_dimension}
173	  \lean{Module.projectiveDimension}
174	  % NOTE: Mathlib pinned at b80f227 may already expose this as
175	  % `Module.projectiveDimension` via
176	  % `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension` /
177	  % `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution`; the prover should re-export
178	  % rather than re-define if the API is present. This block fixes the
179	  % mathematical content the prover must match.
180	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module. The \emph{projective
181	  dimension} of \(M\), written \(\text{pd}_R(M)\), is the infimum in
182	  \(\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}\) of the lengths \(n\) of projective resolutions
183	  \[
184	    0 \longrightarrow P_n \longrightarrow P_{n-1} \longrightarrow \cdots
185	    \longrightarrow P_1 \longrightarrow P_0 \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
186	  \]
187	  with each \(P_i\) a projective \(R\)-module. We say \(M\) has \emph{finite projective dimension}
188	  if \(\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty\), and \emph{infinite projective dimension} otherwise. By
189	  convention \(\text{pd}_R(0) = -\infty\) (some references); we use \(\text{pd}_R(0) = 0\) here
190	  (the empty resolution \(0 \to 0\) of length \(0\)), but the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula in
191	  Theorem~REF concerns only nonzero \(M\).
192	  In the local Noetherian setting a finitely generated \(R\)-module of finite projective
193	  dimension admits a \emph{minimal finite free resolution}: a resolution with each \(P_i\)
194	  free of finite rank and each transition map \(P_{i+1} \to P_i\) landing in \(\mathfrak{m} P_i\).
195	\end{definition}
196	
197	The two basic facts about \(\text{pd}_R(M)\) needed downstream are:
198	\begin{itemize}
199	  \item \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) iff \(M\) is projective (in the local Noetherian setting,
200	    iff \(M\) is free);
201	  \item if \(0 \to K \to P \to M \to 0\) is a short exact sequence with \(P\) projective, then
202	    \(\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_R(K) + 1\) provided \(K \neq 0\).
203	\end{itemize}
204	Both are standard homological algebra and are recorded under
205	\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension\_succ\_iff} or similar in Mathlib.
206	
207	\section{Depth on a short exact sequence}
208	\label{sec:ab_depth_ses}
209	
210	\begin{lemma}
211	
212	\leanok
213	  [Depth on a short exact sequence]
214	  \label{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
215	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact}
216	  
217	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses)
218	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17887-L17920).
219	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
220	  % "Let \(R\) be a local Noetherian ring. Let \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\)
221	  %  be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite \(R\)-modules.
222	  %  \begin{enumerate}
223	  %  \item
224	  %  \(\text{depth}(N) \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N'), \text{depth}(N'')\}\)
225	  %  \item
226	  %  \(\text{depth}(N'') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N') - 1\}\)
227	  %  \item
228	  %  \(\text{depth}(N') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N'') + 1\}\)
229	  %  \end{enumerate}"
230	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses).}
231	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring and let
232	  \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of nonzero finitely generated
233	  \(R\)-modules. Then
234	  \begin{enumerate}
235	    \item \(\text{depth}(N) \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N'),\; \text{depth}(N'')\bigr)\);
236	    \item \(\text{depth}(N'') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N') - 1\bigr)\);
237	    \item \(\text{depth}(N') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N'') + 1\bigr)\).
238	  \end{enumerate}
239	\end{lemma}
240	
241	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
242	% "Use the characterization of depth using the Ext groups
243	%  \(\Ext^i(\kappa, N)\), see Lemma REF,
244	%  and use the long exact cohomology sequence
245	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
246	%  from Lemma REF."
247	\begin{proof}
248	  
249	  
250	  
251	  
252	\leanok
253	  By Lemma~REF, \(\text{depth}(N')\) (resp.\ \(\text{depth}(N)\),
254	  \(\text{depth}(N'')\)) is the smallest index at which \(\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)\) is
255	  nonzero on \(N'\) (resp.\ \(N\), \(N''\)). Apply the long exact \(\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)\)
256	  sequence to \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\):
257	  \[
258	    \cdots \to \text{Ext}^{i-1}_R(\kappa, N'')
259	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N')
260	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N)
261	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N'')
262	    \to \text{Ext}^{i+1}_R(\kappa, N') \to \cdots
263	  \]
264	  Each of the three inequalities is then a direct read-off of the fact that if two
265	  of the three modules in a length-3 chunk vanish then the middle vanishes (for (1)) /
266	  the boundary terms vanish (for (2),(3)). Compare with the diagonal vanishing argument in
267	  the proof of Lemma~REF.
268	\end{proof}

270	\section{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula}
271	\label{sec:ab_main}
272	
273	\begin{theorem}
274	
275	\leanok
276	  [Auslander--Buchsbaum formula]
277	  \label{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
278	  \lean{RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula}
279	  
280	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum)
281	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L27207-L27280).
282	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
283	  % "Let \(R\) be a Noetherian local ring. Let \(M\) be a nonzero finite \(R\)-module
284	  %  which has finite projective dimension \(\text{pd}_R(M)\). Then we have
285	  %  \(\)
286	  %  \text{depth}(R) = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)
287	  %  \(\)"
288	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum); cf.\
289	  Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Theorem 19.1; Auslander--Buchsbaum,
290	  \emph{Homological dimension in local rings}, 1957.}
291	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring and let \(M\) be a nonzero finitely
292	  generated \(R\)-module of finite projective dimension. Then
293	  \[
294	    \text{pd}_R(M) \;+\; \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \text{depth}(R).
295	  \]
296	\end{theorem}
297	
298	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
299	% "We prove this by induction on \(\text{depth}(M)\). The most interesting
300	%  case is the case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\). In this case, let
301	%  \(\)
302	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
303	%  \(\)
304	%  be a minimal finite free resolution, so \(e = \text{pd}_R(M)\).
305	%  By Lemma REF we may assume all matrix
306	%  coefficients of the maps in the complex are contained in the maximal
307	%  ideal of \(R\). Then on the one hand, by
308	%  Proposition REF we see that
309	%  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, breaking the long
310	%  exact sequence into short exact sequences
311	%  \begin{align*}
312	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e - 1}} \to K_{e - 2} \to 0,\\
313	%  0 \to K_{e - 2} \to R^{n_{e - 2}} \to K_{e - 3} \to 0,\\
314	%  \ldots,\\
315	%  0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
316	%  \end{align*}
317	%  we see, using Lemma REF, that
318	%  \begin{align*}
319	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1,\\
320	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2,\\
321	%  \ldots,\\
322	%  \text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e - 1),\\
323	%  \text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e
324	%  \end{align*}
325	%  and since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we conclude \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\).
326	%  This finishes the proof of the case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).
327	%
328	%  Induction step. If \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\), then we pick \(x \in \mathfrak m\)
329	%  which is a nonzerodivisor on both \(M\) and \(R\). This is possible, because
330	%  either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\) and \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the aforementioned
331	%  Proposition REF or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which
332	%  case \(M\) is finite free hence also \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\).
333	%  Thus \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by Lemma REF
334	%  (for example) and we can find an \(x \in \mathfrak m\) which is a nonzerodivisor
335	%  on both \(R\) and \(M\). Let
336	%  \(\)
337	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
338	%  \(\)
339	%  be a minimal resolution as above. An application of the snake lemma
340	%  shows that
341	%  \(\)
342	%  0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to
343	%  M/xM \to 0
344	%  \(\)
345	%  is a minimal resolution too. Thus \(\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\).
346	%  By Lemma REF we have
347	%  \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
348	%  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\).
349	%  Till now depths have all been depths as \(R\) modules, but we observe that
350	%  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) and similarly for \(R/xR\).
351	%  By induction hypothesis we see that the
352	%  Auslander-Buchsbaum formula holds for \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\). Since the
353	%  depths of both \(R/xR\) and \(M/xM\) have decreased by one and the projective
354	%  dimension has not changed we conclude."
355	\begin{proof}
356	  
357	  
358	\leanok
359	  We induct on \(\text{depth}(M)\).
360	
361	  \emph{Base case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).} Let \(e := \text{pd}_R(M)\) and pick a minimal
362	  finite free resolution
363	  \[
364	    0 \longrightarrow R^{n_e} \longrightarrow R^{n_{e-1}} \longrightarrow
365	    \cdots \longrightarrow R^{n_0} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
366	  \]
367	  with all transition maps having matrix coefficients in \(\mathfrak{m}\) (this is the
368	  ``minimal-resolution-after-trimming-trivial-summands'' normalisation, cf.\ Stacks
369	  \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}). On the one hand, the ``what is exact'' criterion
370	  (Stacks tag 00MF, \texttt{proposition-what-exact}, expressing exactness of a sequence of
371	  finite free modules in terms of depths of ideals of \(r\)-minors) gives
372	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, splitting the resolution into short exact
373	  sequences
374	  \[
375	    0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to K_{e-2} \to 0, \quad
376	    0 \to K_{e-2} \to R^{n_{e-2}} \to K_{e-3} \to 0, \quad \dots, \quad
377	    0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0,
378	  \]
379	  and applying Lemma~REF iteratively,
380	  yields the chain of inequalities
381	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1\),
382	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2\), \dots,
383	  \(\text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e-1)\), and finally
384	  \(\text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e\). Since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we deduce
385	  \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\). Combined with the previous inequality, $\text{depth}(R) = e =
386	  \text{pd}_R(M) + 0 = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)$.
387	
388	  \emph{Inductive step \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\).} We claim there exists \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\)
389	  which is a non-zero-divisor on both \(R\) and \(M\). Indeed: either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\), in
390	  which case \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the ``what is exact'' criterion applied to the start
391	  of a minimal resolution, or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which case \(M\) is finite free and
392	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\). Either way both \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) and
393	  \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\), hence \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by
394	  Lemma~REF, so a common non-zero-divisor $x \in
395	  \mathfrak{m}\( exists. Take a minimal finite free resolution of \)M$ as above; the snake
396	  lemma applied to multiplication by \(x\) gives a minimal finite free resolution
397	  \[
398	    0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \cdots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to M/xM \to 0
399	  \]
400	  of \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\), of the same length \(e\). So
401	  \(\text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)\). By Stacks
402	  \texttt{lemma-depth-drops-by-one}, both \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
403	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\), and one verifies
404	  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) (regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}\)
405	  correspond to regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}/(x)\)). Apply the inductive hypothesis to
406	  \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\):
407	  \[
408	    \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) + \text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(R/xR).
409	  \]
410	  Substituting yields
411	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + (\text{depth}(M) - 1) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\), i.e.\
412	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)\), as required.
413	\end{proof}
414	
415	\begin{lemma}
416	
417	\leanok
418	  [Auslander--Buchsbaum: inductive step \(\text{pd} = k+1\)]
419	  \label{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}
420	  \lean{RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}
421	  \uses{thm:auslander_buchsbaum, lem:depth_drops_by_one}
422	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum),
423	  induction-on-\(\text{pd}\) re-organisation; cf.\ Matsumura, Theorem 19.1.}
424	
425	  Inductive-step variant pinning the project-internal helper used by
426	  \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}: for a Noetherian local ring
427	  \((R, \mathfrak m)\), a nonzero finite \(R\)-module \(M\) of projective
428	  dimension \(k+1\), and the AB formula known for projective dimension
429	  \(\leq k\), one has
430	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)\).
431	\end{lemma}
432	\begin{proof}
433	  \uses{lem:depth_drops_by_one}
434	  Replay of the induction step in the main proof's
435	  \emph{inductive step} paragraph (\cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum},
436	  proof block), using \cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one} for the
437	  depth-drop on both \(R\) and \(M\) after quotienting by an
438	  \(M\)-regular \(x \in \mathfrak m\), plus a snake-lemma carving of
439	  the minimal resolution to extract the \(R/(x)\)-minimal resolution
440	  of \(M/xM\) of the same length. The four ingredients are:
441	  \begin{enumerate}
442	    \item Minimal-resolution carving (Stacks
443	      \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}): every finite free
444	      resolution of a finite module over a Noetherian local ring can
445	      be trimmed to a minimal one of the same projective dimension.
446	    \item ``What is exact'' criterion (Stacks 00MF /
447	      \texttt{proposition-what-exact}): characterises exactness in
448	      terms of depths of \(r\)-minors. Used to extract a common
449	      non-zero-divisor \(x \in \mathfrak m\) for both \(R\) and \(M\).
450	    \item Snake lemma applied to multiplication-by-\(x\) on the
451	      minimal resolution: produces a minimal resolution of \(M/xM\)
452	      over \(R/(x)\) of the same length, so
453	      \(\text{pd}_{R/(x)}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)\).
454	    \item Depth drops by one
455	      (\cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one}): once an \(M\)-regular element
456	      \(x \in \mathfrak m\) is fixed,
457	      \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\) and likewise on \(R\).
458	  \end{enumerate}
459	  Combining (1)-(4) with the inductive hypothesis on \(M/xM\) over
460	  \(R/(x)\) closes the inductive step. Pieces (1)-(3) remain Mathlib
461	  gaps as of \texttt{b80f227}; piece (4) is now formalised
462	  axiom-clean (\cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one}).
463	\end{proof}
464

498	The inductive step

506	its purpose is to make the dependency graph and the per-gap effort

510	\paragraph{Sequencing constraint.} The three open gaps are

520	terms of depths of \(r\)-minors of the transition matrices) is
521	independent of gaps (1) and (3) at the substrate level: it is a
522	self-contained criterion on a complex of finite free modules and
523	feeds the closure assembly without going through gap (1) or gap (3).

577	\emph{Cohen--Macaulay Rings}, Construction~1.5.18 together with the

581	Bruns--Herzog Theorem~1.4.13 \(\to\) Eagon--Northcott
582	\(\to\) Buchsbaum--Eisenbud's exactness criterion (Stacks 00MF /
583	\texttt{proposition-what-exact}), which characterises exactness of a
584	complex of finite free modules in terms of depths of the ideals of
585	\(r\)-minors of the transition matrices. Gap (2) is the largest of
586	the three open substrates and is a natural candidate for an upstream
587	Mathlib contribution rather than a project-internal proof. The gap
588	(3) estimate covers the snake-lemma application that converts a

592	\paragraph{Closure assembly.} Once gaps (1) and (3) and gap (2) are
593	all formalised axiom-clean, the body of
594	\cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}
595	(the \(\text{pd}(M) = k+1\) inductive step in
596	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}) closes
597	in an estimated 80--120 LOC of assembly. The assembly proceeds:
598	(a) invoke gap (2) on the start of a minimal finite free resolution
599	of \(M\) to extract a common \(M\)- and \(R\)-regular element
600	\(x \in \mathfrak m\); (b) invoke gap (1) to normalise the
601	minimal-resolution structure; (c) invoke gap (3) on the snake-lemma
602	diagram, obtaining a minimal resolution of \(M/xM\) over \(R/(x)\)
603	of length \(\text{pd}_R(M) = k+1\), so
604	\(\text{pd}_{R/(x)}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)\);
605	(d) apply gap (4)

607	\(M\), to deduce

609	\(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\); (e) close by the
610	inductive hypothesis on \((R/(x), M/xM)\), since
611	\(\text{pd}_{R/(x)}(M/xM) = k+1\) but the induction is on
612	\(\text{depth}(M)\), which has strictly decreased. The arithmetic
613	\(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) - 1 = \text{depth}(R) - 1\)
614	rearranges to the stated formula.

635	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
636	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
637	
638	\begin{definition}
639	
640	\leanok
641	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
642	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
643	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
644	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
645	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
646	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
647	  % "A Noetherian local ring \(R\) is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
648	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
649	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
650	  A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
651	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\) (equivalently, if \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
652	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) means
653	  \(\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)\), cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
654	\end{definition}
655	
656	% NOTE (iter-185 review, supersedes iter-184): the Lean assembly
657	% `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` calls a non-private helper
658	% `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
659	% L1096 — line drifted from iter-184's L944 reference) that now carries a
660	% structural induction scaffold via `regularLocal_inductive_step`, with the
661	% load-bearing sorry pushed into two named helpers: (i)
662	% `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` consolidating the
663	% Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate gaps in one signature, and (ii) an inline
664	% technical-bridge sorry inside `regularLocal_inductive_step` for the
665	% `R⧸(x)`-vs-`QuotSMulTop x R` linear-equiv upgrade (~10-20 LOC iter-186 close
666	% via `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` + `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`).
667	% Neither has a `\lean{...}` pin in this chapter (the lean-vs-blueprint-checker
668	% iter185-auslander minor flagged adding a pin for the public-facing
669	% `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` itself; iter-186+ chapter touch can add
670	% that hint). The Stacks 00NQ Mathlib gap remains the load-bearing substrate;
671	% iter-186+ work paths unchanged: (a) formalise Stacks 00NQ in project
672	% (~300 LOC); (b) Koszul-homology bypass via `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`;
673	% (c) Mathlib upstream.
674	\begin{corollary}
675	
676	\leanok
677	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
678	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
679	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
680	  
681	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
682	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
683	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
684	  % "Let \(R\) be a regular local ring and let
685	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) be a minimal set of generators
686	  %  for the maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\). Then
687	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) is a regular sequence, and
688	  %  each \(R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)\) is a regular local ring
689	  %  of dimension \(d - c\). In particular \(R\) is Cohen-Macaulay."
690	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
691	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is
692	  regular local of Krull dimension \(d\), then \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\).
693	\end{corollary}
694	
695	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
696	% "Note that \(R/x_1R\) is a Noetherian local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\)
697	%  by Lemma REF with \(x_2, \ldots, x_d\)
698	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
699	%  Since \(R\) is a domain by Lemma REF
700	%  \(x_1\) is a nonzerodivisor."
701	\begin{proof}
702	  
703	  
704	  
705	  
706	\leanok
707	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be regular of dimension \(d\) and pick a minimal generating set
708	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) of \(\mathfrak{m}\). By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), \(R\) is
709	  an integral domain, so \(x_1\) is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient \(R/x_1 R\) is a Noetherian
710	  local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\) (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
711	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by \(x_2, \dots, x_d\), hence
712	  has dimension exactly \(d - 1\) and is again regular of dimension \(d - 1\). Inducting on \(d\),
713	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) is an \(R\)-regular sequence, so
714	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq d\). The reverse inequality \(\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d\)
715	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
716	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\), i.e.\
717	  \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay.
718	
719	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
720	  Theorem~REF as follows: take \(M := R\) itself. Then \(M\) is
721	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank \(1\)), so \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\). The
722	  formula gives \(\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)\), a tautology. The non-tautological
723	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension \(d\), every
724	  finitely generated module \(M\) has \(\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d\) (Stacks tag 00O2,
725	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an \(M\) then
726	  relates \(\text{depth}(M)\) to \(d - \text{pd}_R(M)\), which is the form most often used in
727	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
728	  \(R\) Cohen--Macaulay itself.
729	\end{proof}
730	
731	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
732	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
733	
734	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
735	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from a regular projective surface \(S\) to an abelian variety \(A\)
736	across a closed point \(x \in S\) that is missing from the domain of definition \(U \subseteq S\).
737	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
738	
739	\begin{enumerate}
740	  \item The local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
741	    dimension \(2\) (because \(S\) is regular and \(\dim S = 2\)).
742	  \item By Corollary~REF it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
743	    \(\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2\). Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
744	    \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\)-regular sequence of length \(2\) (any two transverse generators).
745	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S\) on \(U = S \setminus \{x\}\) extend
746	    uniquely across \(x\) because \(H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) and
747	    \(H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
748	    \(\geq 2\) at \(x\) by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
749	  \item For a rational map \(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) with codimension-\(\geq 2\) indeterminacy
750	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-\(\geq 2\) input produces a sheaf-theoretic
751	    extension of \(f\) to all of \(S\): pull back any sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_A\)-affine-locally,
752	    obtain sections over \(U\), and extend across \(x\) by the above.
753	\end{enumerate}
754	
755	So Corollary~REF is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
756	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
757	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~REF and
758	Corollary~REF are formalized in
759	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
760	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.

959	      apply hM_top
960	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
961	      intro m _
962	      have hsingle : (Pi.single j m : ι → M) = 0 := habs _ trivial
963	      have heval := congr_fun hsingle j
964	      rwa [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.zero_apply] at heval
965	  | cons r rs' ih =>
966	    intro M _ _
967	    rw [RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff, RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff]
968	    refine and_congr ?_ ?_
969	    · constructor
970	      · intro h
971	        obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
972	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mp h j
973	      · intro h
974	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mpr fun _ => h
975	    · rw [LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr
976	        (quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv (R := R) (ι := ι) r (M := M)) rs']
977	      exact ih (M := QuotSMulTop r M)

979	/-- **Main iter-194 Lane G closure.** For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`,
980	`R`-module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`, the depth of the Pi module
981	`ι → M` equals the depth of `M`:
982	```
983	  depth I (ι → M) = depth I M.
984	```
985	This is the substrate for the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
986	formula: a finite free module `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` has `depth(M) = depth(R)`,
987	so `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` holds. -/
988	lemma depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty
989	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
990	    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
991	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
992	    depth I (ι → M) = depth I M := by
993	  unfold depth
994	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤
995	  · rw [if_pos h, if_pos ((ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mp h)]
996	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt (ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mpr h)]
997	    congr 1
998	    ext n
999	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
1000	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1001	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mp hreg⟩
1002	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1003	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mpr hreg⟩

1005	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): depth drops by exactly one for a regular
1006	element (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).
1007	
1008	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
1009	`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:
1010	`depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`.
1011	
1012	This is one of the four substrate gaps for the Auslander–Buchsbaum n=k+1 step (the
1013	"depth-drops-by-one" piece). The proof packages the long-exact sequence of
1014	`Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`, using that
1015	`[x]` is zero on every `Ext^*(κ, M)` (since `x ∈ Ann κ`) to break the LES into short
1016	exact pieces `0 → Ext^i(κ, M) → Ext^i(κ, M/xM) → Ext^{i+1}(κ, M) → 0`.
1017	
1018	Routed through `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on both sides + the `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`
1019	two-direction bridge that converts a natural-number indexed predicate to a `ℕ∞` inequality. -/
1020	lemma depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular
1021	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1022	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1023	    [Nontrivial M] {x : R} (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1024	    (hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x) :
1025	    depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 =
1026	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
1027	  haveI hMxM_nt : Nontrivial (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1028	    nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
1029	  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (QuotSMulTop x M) := inferInstance
1030	  -- κ as a ModuleCat for compact notation.
1031	  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
1032	  -- x annihilates κ.
1033	  have hxAnnih : x ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) := by
1034	    have h : _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1035	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
1036	    rw [h]; exact hxMem
1037	  -- The SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`.
1038	  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
1039	    ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
1040	  have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
1041	  have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
1042	  -- `[x]_*` is zero on every `Ext^i(κ, M)` via the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`.
1043	  have hSf_kill : ∀ {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) i),
1044	      e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
1045	    intro i e
1046	    have hcomp :
1047	        e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • e := by
1048	      rw [hSf_eq_smul]
1049	      exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ e x).symm
1050	    rw [hcomp]
1051	    exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator e hxAnnih
1052	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
1053	  · -- (≥): depth(M/xM) + 1 ≤ depth M.
1054	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1055	    intro a ha
1056	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
1057	    · subst h0; exact bot_le
1058	    · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
1059	      have hcast : ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
1060	      have hcast' : (Nat.succ n : ℕ) = n + 1 := rfl
1061	      rw [hcast'] at ha
1062	      rw [hcast] at ha
1063	      have hone_ne_top : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤ := by simp
1064	      have hn_le_MxM :
1065	          (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1066	        (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right hone_ne_top).mp ha
1067	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j : ℕ, j < n → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
1068	            (ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop x M)) j, e = 0 :=
1069	        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := QuotSMulTop x M) n).mp hn_le_MxM
1070	      change ((n.succ : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1071	      rw [hcast']
1072	      rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
1073	      intro i hi e
1074	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
1075	      · subst hi0
1076	        -- i = 0 case: x being M-regular ⟹ S.f mono ⟹ postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
1077	        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
1078	          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
1079	          exact hxReg
1080	        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
1081	        apply hinj
1082	        change e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
1083	          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp
1084	              (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
1085	        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
1086	        exact hSf_kill e
1087	      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
1088	        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
1089	          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
1090	        have hj : j < n := by omega
1091	        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
1092	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e (hSf_kill e) rfl
1093	        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
1094	        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
1095	        simpa using hx₃.symm
1096	  · -- (≤): depth M ≤ depth(M/xM) + 1.
1097	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1098	    intro a ha
1099	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
1100	    · subst h0; exact bot_le
1101	    · obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
1102	      have hcast' : (Nat.succ m : ℕ) = m + 1 := rfl
1103	      rw [hcast'] at ha
1104	      have hM_vanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < m + 1 → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
1105	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
1106	        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (m + 1)).mp ha
1107	      have hcast : ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (m : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
1108	      change ((m.succ : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1
1109	      rw [hcast', hcast]
1110	      -- It suffices to show m ≤ depth(M/xM).
1111	      have hMxM_bound :
1112	          (m : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) := by
1113	        rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
1114	        intro j hj e
1115	        -- Use `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at n₀ = j, n₁ = j + 1.
1116	        have he_extClass : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
1117	          hM_vanish (j + 1) (by omega) _
1118	        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
1119	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_extClass
1120	        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hM_vanish j (by omega) _
1121	        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
1122	        simpa using hx₂.symm
1123	      gcongr

1125	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): regular-element existence from positive depth.
1126	
1127	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
1128	`1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ∈ 𝔪`. This
1129	is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
1130	(Nakayama rules out the `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
1131	reading off a length-1 witness.
1132	
1133	This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
1134	two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
1135	element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
1136	lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
1137	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1138	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1139	    [Nontrivial M]
1140	    (h : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
1141	    ∃ x : R, x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular M x := by
1142	  -- Unfold depth: `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
1143	  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
1144	    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
1145	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
1146	  have hdepth_unfold :
1147	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1148	        = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1149	          (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1150	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1151	    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
1152	  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
1153	  have hlt : (0 : ℕ∞) < sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1154	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1155	      ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1156	    calc (0 : ℕ∞) < 1 := by norm_num
1157	      _ ≤ _ := h
1158	  obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
1159	  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ℕ∞) < ↑rs.length).
1160	  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
1161	    by_contra h_neg
1162	    push Not at h_neg
1163	    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp h_neg
1164	    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
1165	    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
1166	    rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
1167	    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
1168	  rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
1169	  · simp at hrs_pos
1170	  · refine ⟨x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_⟩
1171	    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1

1173	/-! ### Helper iter-199 Lane AB-gap1 (axiom-clean): minimal surjection substrate.
1174	
1175	For a finite `R`-module `M` over a local ring `R`, there exists a surjective
1176	`R`-linear map `f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M` of the **minimal possible rank**
1177	`n = dim_κ (κ ⊗_R M)` (where `κ = R/𝔪` is the residue field) whose **kernel
1178	is contained in `𝔪 • ⊤`**. This is the first step of constructing a *minimal
1179	finite free resolution*: iterating the construction on the kernel (which is
1180	itself finitely generated when `R` is Noetherian) produces successive
1181	syzygies whose differential maps each have image in `𝔪` times their target.
1182	
1183	This is the first substrate piece of gap (1) (Stacks
1184	`lemma-add-trivial-complex`) in the Auslander–Buchsbaum closure chain
1185	(`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`). It packages the basic
1186	**Nakayama-lift** of a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M` to an `R`-spanning family in `M`
1187	and reads off the kernel-containment from linear independence of the basis
1188	combined with the `1 ⊗_R -` evaluation.
1189	
1190	Mathlib substrate used (all axiom-clean):
1191	* `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` — Nakayama lift of a κ-basis.
1192	* `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` — the `1 ⊗_R -` map is surjective for the
1193	  residue-field tensor.
1194	* `Module.Basis.constr_range` — range of the linear extension equals span of
1195	  the chosen image set.
1196	* `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` — independence of a κ-basis.
1197	* `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` — `r ∈ 𝔪 ↔ residue r = 0`. -/
1198	lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
1199	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1200	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
1201	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
1202	      Function.Surjective f ∧
1203	      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1204	        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ∧
1205	      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
1206	  set κ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hκ
1207	  set n := _root_.Module.finrank κ (TensorProduct R κ M) with hn
1208	  -- Pick a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M`.
1209	  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (TensorProduct R κ M) :=
1210	    _root_.Module.finBasis κ (TensorProduct R κ M)
1211	  -- The canonical map `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` is surjective.
1212	  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
1213	    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
1214	    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1215	  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
1216	  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
1217	  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
1218	  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
1219	  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n → R` to `m i`.
1220	  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
1221	  -- Evaluation: `f x = Σ x i • m i`.
1222	  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
1223	    intro x
1224	    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
1225	        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
1226	    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
1227	      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
1228	    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
1229	    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
1230	  -- Range = span of `m`.
1231	  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
1232	    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
1233	  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
1234	  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
1235	    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
1236	  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
1237	  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
1238	  · -- Kernel containment in `𝔪 • ⊤`.
1239	    intro x hx
1240	    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
1241	    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
1242	    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.
1243	    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R κ M) := by
1244	      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
1245	    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
1246	    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ⊗_R (x i • m i) = residue(x i) • b i`.
1247	    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
1248	        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
1249	      intro i
1250	      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1251	          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
1252	        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
1253	      rw [hm i]; rfl
1254	    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1255	        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
1256	      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
1257	    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
1258	    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
1259	    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
1260	      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
1261	        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
1262	      exact fun i => this i
1263	    -- Convert each component-in-𝔪 to `x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
1264	    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
1265	      intro i
1266	      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
1267	      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
1268	    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
1269	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
1270	    intro i _
1271	    have hsingle :
1272	        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
1273	          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
1274	      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
1275	    rw [hsingle]
1276	    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial
1277	
1278	end Module

1280	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1281	
1282	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1283	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1284	```
1285	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1286	```
1287	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1288	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1289	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1290	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1291	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1292	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1293	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1294	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1295	
1296	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1297	
1298	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1299

1317	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1318	
1319	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1320	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1321	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1322	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1323	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1324	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1325	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1326	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1327	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1328	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1329	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.

1332	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1333	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1334	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1335	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1336	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1337	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and

1341	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1342	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.

1435	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective

1452	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
1453	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1454	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1455	    [Nontrivial M]

1458	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1459	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
1460	  -- iter-193 Lane G: structural scaffold for Stacks 090V. We split on `n` to
1461	  -- isolate the base case `pd_R(M) = 0` (where `M` is finite free over a
1462	  -- Noetherian local ring) from the inductive step `pd_R(M) = k + 1` (which
1463	  -- inducts on `depth(M)` via the snake-lemma-on-minimal-resolution recipe).
1464	  --
1465	  -- The `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper above (axiom-clean) is the substrate
1466	  -- piece used in the `n = 0` case to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)`
1467	  -- for `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)`; the residual `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` step is
1468	  -- the remaining substrate gap (a single direct-sum-of-modules depth fact).
1469	  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
1470	  · -- **Base case `n = 0`**: `pd_R(M) = 0` ⟹ `M` projective ⟹ (finite + local)
1471	    -- `M` free ⟹ `depth(M) = depth(R)`. The formula
1472	    -- `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` collapses to `depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1473	    subst hn0
1474	    rw [Nat.cast_zero, zero_add]
1475	    -- Step 1: unfold `_root_.Module.projectiveDimension` to the categorical
1476	    -- form, then apply `projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff`.
1477	    have hpd' :
1478	        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) = (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
1479	      unfold _root_.Module.projectiveDimension at _hpd
1480	      exact _hpd
1481	    obtain ⟨hproj, _hNonZero⟩ :=
1482	      (CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff _).mp hpd'
1483	    -- Step 2: `CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R M)` ⟹ `Module.Projective R M`.
1484	    have hMproj : _root_.Module.Projective R M :=
1485	      (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mpr hproj
1486	    -- Step 3: `Module.Projective` ⟹ `Module.Flat`.
1487	    haveI : _root_.Module.Flat R M := _root_.Module.Flat.of_projective
1488	    -- Step 4: `Module.Flat` + `IsLocalRing` + `Module.Finite` ⟹ `Module.Free`.

1491	    -- `depth(M) = depth(R)` via the `Module.finBasis` equivalence and the
1492	    -- axiom-clean `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper. The residual is the
1493	    -- single, narrower statement: `depth(Fin k → R) = depth(R)` for `k ≥ 1`.
1494	    have hk : 0 < _root_.Module.finrank R M :=
1495	      (_root_.Module.finrank_pos_iff_of_free R M).mpr inferInstance
1496	    set k : ℕ := _root_.Module.finrank R M with hk_def
1497	    -- Build the equivalence `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)` via the chosen basis.
1498	    let e : M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R) := (_root_.Module.finBasis R M).equivFun
1499	    -- Transport `depth(M) = depth(Fin k → R)` using the axiom-clean helper.
1500	    have hdepth_M_eq : Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1501	        = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin k → R) :=
1502	      Module.depth_eq_of_linearEquiv _ e
1503	    rw [hdepth_M_eq]

1505	    -- `Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty` which proves the substrate
1506	    -- `depth(ι → M) = depth(M)` for nonempty finite `ι` (Pi-quotient
1507	    -- decomposition + regular-sequence transport).

1511	    -- structural-carving helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`,

1518	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
1519	
1520	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
1521	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
1522	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
1523	
1524	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
1525	
1526	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
1527	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
1528	
1529	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
1530	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
1531	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
1532	this is the upstream gap-fill.
1533	
1534	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
1535	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
1536	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
1537	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
1538	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1539	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
1540	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
1541	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
1542	  depth_eq_krullDim :
1543	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
1544	
1545	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
1546	
1547	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
1548	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
1549	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
1550	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
1551	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
1552	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
1553	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
1554	
1555	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
1556	
1557	namespace CohenMacaulay
1558	
1559	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
1560	
1561	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
1562	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
1563	specialisation of the equality
1564	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
1565	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
1566	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
1567	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
1568	
1569	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
1570	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
1571	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1572	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
1573	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
1574	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
1575	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
1576	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
1577	    intro habs
1578	    apply h.top_ne_smul
1579	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
1580	    rw [habs]; simp
1581	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
1582	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
1583	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
1584	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
1585	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
1586	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq
1587	
1588	/-! ### Helper 1.5 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent-image of `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`
1589	
1590	For a local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`, the image of `x` in the
1591	cotangent space `𝔪.Cotangent` is nonzero. This is the key positivity input
1592	for the iter-187 cotangent dim-drop lemma below.
1593	
1594	Axiom-clean: direct from `Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero`. -/
1595	private lemma toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq
1596	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1597	    (x : R) (hx : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1598	    (hxnotsq : x ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ^ 2) :
1599	    (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
1600	        (⟨x, hx⟩ : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Ideal R)) ≠ 0 := by
1601	  intro habs
1602	  exact hxnotsq
1603	    ((Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero (I := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ⟨x, hx⟩).mp habs)
1604	
1605	/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`
1606	
1607	**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
1608	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
1609	```
1610	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
1611	```
1612	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
1613	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
1614	
1615	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
1616	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
1617	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
1618	`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
1619	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
1620	
1621	**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
1622	1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
1623	   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
1624	   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
1625	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
1626	2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
1627	   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
1628	   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
1629	   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
1630	3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
1631	   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
1632	4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
1633	   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.
1634	5. Combining via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` and the
1635	   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
1636	   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by

1899	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
1900	
1901	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
1902	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
1903	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
1904	
1905	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
1906	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
1907	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
1908	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
1909	   `lemma-one-equation`).
1910	
1911	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
1912	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
1913	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
1914	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
1915	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
1916	
1917	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
1918	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
1919	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
1920	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
1921	
1922	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
1923	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
1924	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
1925	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
1926	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
1927	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
1928	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
1929	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
1930	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
1931	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
1932	helper closes. -/

1934	/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain
1935	
1936	The Auslander–Buchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay"
1937	ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
1938	is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose
1939	this implication (`lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is a domain"
1940	returns nothing relevant; `lean_loogle` for `IsRegularLocalRing _ → IsDomain _`
1941	returns empty).
1942	
1943	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
1944	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
1945	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
1946	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
1947	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
1948	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
1949	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
1950	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
1951	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
1952	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
1953	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
1954	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
1955	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
1956	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
1957	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
1958	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
1959	
1960	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
1961	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter
1962	or Mathlib upstream contribution. -/

1964	/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
1965	with `spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1`, there exists `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
1966	
1967	This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪²`
1968	then `𝔪 = 0` (so `spanFinrank 𝔪 = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
1969	private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
1970	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1971	    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
1972	    ∃ x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
1973	  -- By contradiction: assume 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪², then by Nakayama 𝔪 = 0, but spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1.
1974	  by_contra h_neg
1975	  push Not at h_neg
1976	  have h𝔪_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
1977	  -- 𝔪² = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
1978	  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
1979	      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
1980	    rw [sq, ← Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
1981	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
1982	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
1983	  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ⊥.
1984	  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ≤ (⊥ : Ideal R).jacobson :=
1985	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
1986	  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
1987	      ≤ ⊥ ⊔ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) •
1988	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
1989	    rw [bot_sup_eq, ← hsq]; exact h𝔪_le_sq
1990	  have h𝔪_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ≤ ⊥ :=
1991	    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot h𝔪_fg hjac h_le_smul
1992	  have h𝔪_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ⊥ := le_bot_iff.mp h𝔪_bot
1993	  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
1994	    rw [h𝔪_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
1995	  omega

1997	/-! ### Helper for Stacks 00NQ base case (iter-190 Lane G, axiom-clean).
1998	
1999	For a Noetherian local ring `R` with `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0`, the
2000	ring `R` is a field, hence a domain. The maximal ideal collapses to `⊥` via
2001	`Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot` (under FG), and
2002	`IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` upgrades the resulting field-by-
2003	trivial-maximal-ideal characterisation to `IsField R`, from which
2004	`IsField.isDomain` gives `IsDomain R`. -/
2005	private lemma isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero
2006	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2007	    (h : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0) : IsDomain R := by
2008	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).FG :=
2009	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
2010	  have h𝔪_bot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R = ⊥ :=
2011	    (Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot h𝔪_fg).mp h
2012	  have hField : IsField R :=
2013	    IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr h𝔪_bot
2014	  exact hField.isDomain

2016	/-! ### Helper for Stacks 00NU inductive step prep (iter-190 Lane G, axiom-clean).
2017	
2018	For a regular local Noetherian ring `R` of `spanFinrank 𝔪 = k + 1` and
2019	`x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the quotient `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` is again a regular local
2020	ring of `spanFinrank 𝔪' = k`.
2021	
2022	This is the **axiom-clean** counterpart of
2023	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`: it avoids the
2024	`IsSMulRegular R x` hypothesis (which depends on `isDomain_of_regularLocal`)
2025	by routing the dimension lower bound through
2026	`ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard` — a Krull-height bound
2027	that does NOT require `x` to be a non-zero-divisor — instead of
2028	`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim`. -/
2029	private lemma regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq
2030	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2031	    [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2032	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2033	    (x : R) (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2034	    (hxNotSq : x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2) :
2035	    ∃ _ : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2036	    ∃ _ : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2037	    ∃ _ : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2038	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
2039	  -- Step 1: instances on R/(x).
2040	  have hxNonunit : ¬ IsUnit x := fun hu =>
2041	    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem
2042	  have hspan_ne_top : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≠ ⊤ :=
2043	    Ideal.span_singleton_ne_top hxNonunit
2044	  haveI hNT : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2045	    Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hspan_ne_top
2046	  haveI hLR : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2047	    IsLocalRing.of_surjective' (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2048	  -- Step 2: cotangent dim drop → spanFinrank drop on R/(x).
2049	  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ x hxMem hxNotSq
2050	  have hR_cot_eq :
2051	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
2052	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) = k + 1 := by
2053	    rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
2054	    exact hdim
2055	  have hR'_cot_eq :
2056	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
2057	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by
2058	    have h := hcot
2059	    rw [hR_cot_eq] at h
2060	    omega
2061	  have hspan_R'_eq_k :
2062	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
2063	    rw [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace
2064	        (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))]
2065	    exact hR'_cot_eq
2066	  -- Step 3: Krull height theorem: ringKrullDim R ≤ ringKrullDim R/(x) + 1.
2067	  have hxJac : x ∈ Ring.jacobson R := by
2068	    rw [IsLocalRing.ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal]
2069	    exact hxMem
2070	  have h_x_subset_jac : ({x} : Set R) ⊆ Ring.jacobson R := by
2071	    intro y hy
2072	    rcases hy with rfl
2073	    exact hxJac
2074	  have hKrullDimLE : ringKrullDim R ≤
2075	      ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1 := by
2076	    have h := ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard
2077	                ({x} : Set R) h_x_subset_jac
2078	    simpa using h
2079	  have hR_dim : ringKrullDim R = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2080	    have h := IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal (R := R)
2081	    rw [hdim] at h
2082	    exact_mod_cast h.symm
2083	  -- Step 4: extract `ringKrullDim R/(x) = k` from hKrullDimLE + upper bound.
2084	  -- Upper bound: `ringKrullDim R/(x) ≤ spanFinrank 𝔪' = k`.
2085	  have h_dim_upper : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≤
2086	      ((k : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2087	    refine le_trans (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal _) ?_
2088	    rw [hspan_R'_eq_k]
2089	  -- Adding 1 to both sides of h_dim_upper:
2090	  -- `ringKrullDim R/(x) + 1 ≤ (k:WithBot) + 1 = (k+1:WithBot) = ringKrullDim R`.
2091	  have h_add_le : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1 ≤
2092	      ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2093	    calc ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1
2094	        ≤ ((k : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) + 1 := by gcongr
2095	      _ = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by push_cast; ring
2096	  -- Combined with hKrullDimLE via hR_dim → equation in WithBot.
2097	  have h_add_eq : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1
2098	      = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2099	    rw [hR_dim] at hKrullDimLE
2100	    exact le_antisymm h_add_le hKrullDimLE
2101	  -- Extract `ringKrullDim R/(x) = k : WithBot ℕ∞` via `WithBot.add_eq_coe`.
2102	  have hR'_dim_eq : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))
2103	      = ((k : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2104	    obtain ⟨a', b', ha', hb', hab⟩ := WithBot.add_eq_coe.mp h_add_eq
2105	    rw [← ha']
2106	    have hb_eq : b' = (1 : ℕ∞) := by
2107	      have h1 : ((b' : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) = ((1 : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2108	        rw [hb']; simp
2109	      exact_mod_cast h1
2110	    have ha_eq : a' = (k : ℕ∞) := by
2111	      rw [hb_eq] at hab
2112	      have hcast2 : a' + 1 = (k : ℕ∞) + 1 := by exact_mod_cast hab
2113	      have hne_top : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤ := by simp
2114	      exact WithTop.add_right_cancel hne_top hcast2
2115	    exact_mod_cast ha_eq
2116	  -- Step 5: spanFinrank 𝔪' = k = ringKrullDim R/(x) → IsRegularLocalRing R/(x).
2117	  have hRLR : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) := by
2118	    apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
2119	    rw [hspan_R'_eq_k, hR'_dim_eq]
2120	  exact ⟨hNT, hLR, hRLR, hspan_R'_eq_k⟩

2122	/-! ### Helper iter-191 Lane G (axiom-clean): zero-divisor witness from a minimal prime
2123	
2124	For a commutative ring `R` and a minimal prime `𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R`, every
2125	element of `𝔭` is a zero-divisor in `R`. Concretely: for any `x ∈ 𝔭`, there
2126	exists `y ∈ R, y ≠ 0` with `x * y = 0`.
2127	
2128	Proof: minimal primes are disjoint from non-zero-divisors via
2129	`Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes`, so `x ∈ 𝔭` ⟹
2130	`x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R` ⟹ `∃ y ≠ 0, x * y = 0`.
2131	
2132	This packages the Stacks-00NQ-relevant first step for the `x ∈ 𝔭` case of
2133	`isDomain_of_regularLocal`: when `(x)` is a minimal prime, `x` is a zero-divisor,
2134	so the obstruction reduces to derivation of a contradiction from the
2135	zero-divisor witness (using the regular-local structure of `R` and `R/(x)`). -/
2136	private lemma exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes
2137	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {𝔭 : Ideal R} (h𝔭 : 𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R)
2138	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ 𝔭) :
2139	    ∃ y : R, y ≠ 0 ∧ x * y = 0 := by
2140	  have hdisj : Disjoint (𝔭 : Set R) (nonZeroDivisors R : Set R) :=
2141	    Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes h𝔭
2142	  have hxNot : x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R := fun hxNZD =>
2143	    (Set.disjoint_left.mp hdisj) hx hxNZD
2144	  have hExistsZD : ¬ ∀ z, x * z = 0 → z = 0 := fun h => hxNot <| by
2145	    rw [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff]
2146	    refine ⟨h, fun z hz => h z (by rw [mul_comm]; exact hz)⟩
2147	  push Not at hExistsZD
2148	  obtain ⟨y, hxy, hy⟩ := hExistsZD
2149	  exact ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩
